is a [cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") and sports club in the [Stockbridge](/wiki/Stockbridge,_Edinburgh "Stockbridge, Edinburgh") district of [Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh "Edinburgh"), [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"). The cricket ground, commonly known as **The Grange**, is the regular home of the [Scotland national cricket team](/wiki/Scotland_national_cricket_team "Scotland national cricket team"), and is situated adjacent to the [Edinburgh Academy](/wiki/Edinburgh_Academy "Edinburgh Academy") sports ground, which is in [Raeburn Place](/wiki/Raeburn_Place "Raeburn Place").

– [The Grange Cricket Club](/w/index.php?title=The_Grange_Cricket_Club&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Grange Cricket Club (page does not exist)") is an amateur cricket club whose 1st XI play in the Cricket Scotland Eastern Premiership, the top tier of cricket for teams of the [East of Scotland Cricket Association](/wiki/East_of_Scotland_Cricket_Association "East of Scotland Cricket Association") (ESCA) and [Strathmore & Perthshire Cricket Union](/wiki/Strathmore_%26_Perthshire_Cricket_Union "Strathmore & Perthshire Cricket Union") regional associations. The club 2nd XI play in the ESCA [Baillie Gifford](/wiki/Baillie_Gifford "Baillie Gifford") Championship Division. The Grange Cricket Club has won the Scottish Cup six times, the East of Scotland League (1953–1996) fourteen times and the Scottish National Cricket League (1997–present) five times.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

– As of 2016 The Grange Club has also been one of the home grounds for the [Eastern Knights](/wiki/Eastern_Knights "Eastern Knights"), who play in the [Regional Pro Series](/wiki/Regional_Pro_Series "Regional Pro Series"), the highest, and only professional, tier of Scottish cricket.

– The Grange is perhaps most importantly home to the [Scotland national cricket team](/wiki/Scotland_national_cricket_team "Scotland national cricket team"), who represent Scotland for cricket in international matches.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Cricket and sports club in the Stockbridge district of Edinburgh, Scotland

This article is about a cricket club. For the suburb of Edinburgh called The Grange, see [The Grange, Edinburgh](/wiki/The_Grange,_Edinburgh "The Grange, Edinburgh").

**The Grange Club** is a [cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") and sports club in the [Stockbridge](/wiki/Stockbridge,_Edinburgh "Stockbridge, Edinburgh") district of [Edinburgh](/wiki/Edinburgh "Edinburgh"), [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland"). The cricket ground, commonly known as **The Grange**, is the regular home of the [Scotland national cricket team](/wiki/Scotland_national_cricket_team "Scotland national cricket team"), and is situated adjacent to the [Edinburgh Academy](/wiki/Edinburgh_Academy "Edinburgh Academy") sports ground, which is in [Raeburn Place](/wiki/Raeburn_Place "Raeburn Place").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Grange_Club&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The Grange Club was founded in 1832, in [The Grange](/wiki/The_Grange,_Edinburgh "The Grange, Edinburgh") district of Edinburgh. In 1872 it moved to its current location at Raeburn Place in the Stockbridge district and has hosted out of its pavilion since 1893. The pavilion cost £1,400 and was officially opened on 29 June 1893 by Lord Moncrieff. The pavilion was restored in 1998 at a cost of £450,000.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

After the Scottish Cricket Union disbanded in 1883 The Grange Club assumed responsibility as the governing body of cricket in Scotland for a time and still holds considerable national influence.

The decorative scheme to the interior of the Pavilion is designed to complement the exterior. The Long Room, is modelled on the [Marylebone Cricket Club's](/wiki/Marylebone_Cricket_Club "Marylebone Cricket Club") 'Long Room' at [Lord's Cricket Ground](/wiki/Lord%27s "Lord's"), London.

The club was also associated with [The Dyvours Club](/wiki/The_Dyvours_Club "The Dyvours Club"), Edinburgh's oldest [lawn tennis](/wiki/Lawn-Tennis "Lawn-Tennis") club, who were founded in 1883, and played on the grounds.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Cricket

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Grange_Club&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cricket")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/ec/Pavilion%2C_Grange_Cricket_Club_%28geograph_6958212%29.jpg/250px-Pavilion%2C_Grange_Cricket_Club_%28geograph_6958212%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Pavilion,_Grange_Cricket_Club_(geograph_6958212).jpg)

View of the clubhouse, 2021

The Grange has hosted numerous high-profile international matches over the years featuring teams such as [Australia](/wiki/Australia_national_cricket_team "Australia national cricket team"), [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan_national_cricket_team "Pakistan national cricket team"), [England](/wiki/England_cricket_team "England cricket team") and [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand_national_cricket_team "New Zealand national cricket team"). Some of the world's finest cricketers have played at The Grange, from [W. G. Grace](/wiki/W._G._Grace "W. G. Grace") in 1895 and [Donald Bradman](/wiki/Donald_Bradman "Donald Bradman") in 1948 to [Brian Lara](/wiki/Brian_Lara "Brian Lara") in 1995, [Shane Warne](/wiki/Shane_Warne "Shane Warne") and [Andrew Flintoff](/wiki/Andrew_Flintoff "Andrew Flintoff"). The ground has hosted Scotland's home matches in ECB domestic cricket competitions.

International venue

The Grange hosted Scotland's first official [One Day International](/wiki/One_Day_International "One Day International") (ODI) outside of a [Cricket World Cup](/wiki/Cricket_World_Cup "Cricket World Cup") on 27 June 2006. A capacity crowd saw Scotland lose by five wickets to Pakistan. It was selected as a venue to host matches in the [2015 ICC World Twenty20 Qualifier](/wiki/2015_ICC_World_Twenty20_Qualifier "2015 ICC World Twenty20 Qualifier") tournament.[[3]](#cite_note-2015Fixtures-3)

Clubs

The Grange Club is the home ground for:

* **The Grange Cricket Club** – [The Grange Cricket Club](/w/index.php?title=The_Grange_Cricket_Club&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Grange Cricket Club (page does not exist)") is an amateur cricket club whose 1st XI play in the Cricket Scotland Eastern Premiership, the top tier of cricket for teams of the [East of Scotland Cricket Association](/wiki/East_of_Scotland_Cricket_Association "East of Scotland Cricket Association") (ESCA) and [Strathmore & Perthshire Cricket Union](/wiki/Strathmore_%26_Perthshire_Cricket_Union "Strathmore & Perthshire Cricket Union") regional associations. The club 2nd XI play in the ESCA [Baillie Gifford](/wiki/Baillie_Gifford "Baillie Gifford") Championship Division. The Grange Cricket Club has won the Scottish Cup six times, the East of Scotland League (1953–1996) fourteen times and the Scottish National Cricket League (1997–present) five times.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
* **The Eastern Knights** – As of 2016 The Grange Club has also been one of the home grounds for the [Eastern Knights](/wiki/Eastern_Knights "Eastern Knights"), who play in the [Regional Pro Series](/wiki/Regional_Pro_Series "Regional Pro Series"), the highest, and only professional, tier of Scottish cricket.
* **Scotland national cricket team** – The Grange is perhaps most importantly home to the [Scotland national cricket team](/wiki/Scotland_national_cricket_team "Scotland national cricket team"), who represent Scotland for cricket in international matches.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Cricket World Cup

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Grange_Club&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Cricket World Cup")]

The Grange hosted two ODIs during the [1999 Cricket World Cup](/wiki/1999_Cricket_World_Cup "1999 Cricket World Cup").

| 24 May 1999  [Scorecard](http://www.espncricinfo.com/ci/engine/match/65209.html) |

| [Minhajul Abedin](/wiki/Minhajul_Abedin "Minhajul Abedin") 68\* (116)  [John Blain](/wiki/John_Blain_(cricketer) "John Blain (cricketer)") 4/37 (10 overs) |  | [Gavin Hamilton](/wiki/Gavin_Hamilton_(cricketer) "Gavin Hamilton (cricketer)") 63 (71)  [Hasibul Hossain](/wiki/Hasibul_Hossain "Hasibul Hossain") 2/26 (8 overs) |

## Scotland v England June 2018

See also: [English cricket team in Scotland in 2018](/wiki/English_cricket_team_in_Scotland_in_2018 "English cricket team in Scotland in 2018")

### Only ODI

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Grange_Club&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Only ODI")]

|  |
| --- |
| 10 June 2018  11:00  [Scorecard](http://www.espncricinfo.com/ci/engine/match/1124639.html) |

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **[Scotland](/wiki/Scotland_national_cricket_team "Scotland national cricket team")** 371/5 (50 overs) | **v** | **[England](/wiki/England_cricket_team "England cricket team")** 365 (48.5 overs) |
| [Calum MacLeod](/wiki/Calum_MacLeod_(cricketer) "Calum MacLeod (cricketer)") 140[\*](/wiki/Not_out "Not out") (94)  [Adil Rashid](/wiki/Adil_Rashid "Adil Rashid") 2/72 (10 overs) |  | [Jonny Bairstow](/wiki/Jonny_Bairstow "Jonny Bairstow") 105 (59)  [Mark Watt](/wiki/Mark_Watt "Mark Watt") 3/55 (10) |

|  |
| --- |
| **Scotland won by 6 runs**  Grange Cricket Club Ground, Edinburgh  Umpires: [Marais Erasmus](/wiki/Marais_Erasmus "Marais Erasmus") (SA) and [Allan Haggo](/wiki/Allan_Haggo "Allan Haggo") (Sco)  Player of the match: [Calum MacLeod](/wiki/Calum_MacLeod_(cricketer) "Calum MacLeod (cricketer)") (Sco) |

* England won the toss and elected to field.
* [Dylan Budge](/wiki/Dylan_Budge "Dylan Budge") (Sco) made his ODI debut.
* *[Calum MacLeod](/wiki/Calum_MacLeod_(cricketer) "Calum MacLeod (cricketer)") scored the fastest century by a batsman for Scotland in ODIs*[[6]](#cite_note-result-6) and became the first batsman for Scotland to score a century in ODIs against England.[[7]](#cite_note-Stats-7)
* *Scotland made their highest score in ODIs and the highest score by an [Associate](/wiki/List_of_International_Cricket_Council_members#Associate_members "List of International Cricket Council members") team against a [Full Member](/wiki/List_of_International_Cricket_Council_members#Full_members "List of International Cricket Council members") team.*[[7]](#cite_note-Stats-7)
* *[Jonny Bairstow](/wiki/Jonny_Bairstow "Jonny Bairstow") became the first batsman for England to score centuries in three consecutive ODIs.*[[7]](#cite_note-Stats-7)
* *Scotland win for the first time against England in any format.*[[7]](#cite_note-Stats-7)

## Squash, tennis and hockey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Grange_Club&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Squash, tennis and hockey")]

The Grange also hosts other sports besides cricket. It has five squash courts, which support men's and ladies' teams that compete at all regional and national levels. Uniquely for a private club in Scotland, The Grange is also home to [The Dyvours Club](/wiki/The_Dyvours_Club "The Dyvours Club") which has four grass tennis courts and four floodlit [astroturf](/wiki/AstroTurf "AstroTurf") courts. [Grange Hockey Club](/wiki/Grange_Hockey_Club "Grange Hockey Club") supports eight men's hockey teams which represents a broad range of ability. The 1st XI recently played in Europe, having won the Scottish Cup, and also play in the [Euro Hockey League](/wiki/Euro_Hockey_League "Euro Hockey League"). The Grange Club is also home to Grange Edinburgh Ladies Hockey Club with four teams. All the constituent clubs have vibrant junior sections.
